rulings.Case Title: ESTATE OF FRANKLIN GOLDBERG AND JUNA GOLDBERG VS. REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Deny Defts' Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative To Continue The Hearing On The Motion For Summary Judgment And For Sanctions ; Memorandum Of Point And Authorities, Request For Judicial Notice,
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 23, 2011, Line 16, PLAINTIFF GOLDBERG, JUNA, AS THE PERSONAL REPRESENTATIVE OF, JUNA GOLDBERG'S Motion For An Order To Deny Defendants' Motion For Summary Judgment Or In The Alternative To Continue The Hearing On The Motion For Summary Judgment And For Sanctions is Granted in part. As an accommodation to Ms. Rajender and without addressing the plaintiffs¿ purported need to take additional discovery, the hearing date on the motion for summary judgment is continued to January 11, 2012. Opposition papers are due 14 calendar days before the hearing and reply papers are due 5 calendar days before the hearing. Absent an unanticipated emergency, plaintiffs may not, prior to the date they file their opposition papers, seek a further continuance of the hearing. However, plaintiffs, if they have good cause to do so, are not precluded from making a CCP 437c(h) request for a continuance as part of their opposition papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: ANGELA BURTON VS. SAFEWAY INC. et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday, November 23, 2011, Line 6, DEFENDANT SAFEWAY INCORPORATED'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS Denied. Defendant failed to satisfy its summary judgment burden negating that it had constructive notice of the grape on the floor that caused the incident. Even if true, defendant¿s fact 18 is insufficient to establish that defendant inspected the area where plaintiff fell within a reasonable period of the incident. (See Ortega v. Kmart Corp. (2001) 26 Cal.4th 1200). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

Off calendar as moot as to Defendants American International Group, Inc., Chartis, Inc. and Chartis U.S., Inc. in light of the ruling on the Motion to Quash. Overruled as to remaining Defendants. The other causes of action are properly pled.
